name: Irish Loin of Pork with Lemon and Herbs
recipeInstructions:
1. Pat the pork dry with a paper towel.
2. Score the surface of the pork generously using a sharp knife.
3. In a small bowl, combine parsley, onion, lemon peel, basil, and garlic.
4. Whisk in 2/3 of the olive oil until well combined.
5. Rub the mixture evenly over the pork.
6. Wrap the pork tightly in foil and refrigerate overnight.
7. Allow the pork to come to room temperature for about an hour before roasting.
8. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
9. Brush the remaining olive oil over the pork.
10. Place the pork on a rack in a shallow pan.
11. Roast until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the meat reads 170 degrees Fahrenheit, approximately 2 and a half hours.
12. Let the meat rest.
13. Strain the pan juices to remove excess fat.
14. Mix the strained juices with the dry sherry.
15. Cook over low heat for about 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
16. Pour the sauce into a serving dish.
17. Transfer the pork to a platter.
18. Garnish with fresh parsley and lemon slices.
19. Serve the sauce separately.
